query: How hard would it be to move my EKS cluster off the nginx ingress controller? Score it for me.
Run an ingress migration assessment for account 123456789012 — I want to know what it takes to reach Gateway API.
We're on ingress-nginx and want to migrate to the AWS Load Balancer Controller. Assess our setup and what changes.
Audit my ingress controllers and tell me which routes move cleanly to ALB and which need a redesign.
Plan our nginx ingress retirement — what breaks, what's a clean annotation swap, what needs re-architecture?
Map our nginx Ingress resources to HTTPRoute and flag the ones with no Gateway API equivalent.
Generate ready-to-apply Gateway API and ALB manifests from our current nginx ingresses.
Give me a migration difficulty score for leaving nginx ingress on the prod cluster.
Can AWS Transform (ATX) auto-convert our nginx ingress annotations to ALB? Assess it.
Run an EKS operational review on payment-prod — a full GREEN/AMBER/RED audit across all 10 sections.
Audit my cluster's operational posture and give me a maturity score with prioritized actions.
What version of EKS am I running, and which add-ons and compute strategy do we have? Just inherited the cluster.
Do a full reconnaissance of our EKS environment — IaC tooling, CI/CD, and observability stack.
Is my cluster ready to upgrade from 1.31 to 1.32? Give me a readiness score and the blockers.
What deprecated APIs will break when I move to the next Kubernetes version?
Should we use Karpenter or Managed Node Groups for our new EKS cluster?
What's the best practice for Pod Security Standards and network policies on EKS?
